label: 20311 Technology pilot program

children.heading: Absent uniformed services voter
children.label: 1 Absent uniformed services voter
children.text: The term “absent uniformed services voter” has the meaning given such term in section 107(1) of the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act () [now ].42 U.S.C. 1973ff–6(1)52 U.S.C. 20310(1)

children.heading: Overseas voter
children.label: 2 Overseas voter
children.text: The term “overseas voter” has the meaning given such term in section 107(5) of such Act [].52 U.S.C. 20310(5)

children.heading: Presidential designee
children.label: 3 Presidential designee
children.text: The term “Presidential designee” means the individual designated under section 101(a) of such Act [].52 U.S.C. 20301(a)

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Presidential designee may establish 1 or more pilot programs under which the feasibility of new election technology is tested for the benefit of absent uniformed services voters and overseas voters claiming rights under the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act ( et seq.) [now et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1973ff52 U.S.C. 20301

children.label: A
children.text: shall be at the discretion of the Presidential designee; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall not conflict with or substitute for existing laws, regulations, or procedures with respect to the participation of absent uniformed services voters and military voters in elections for Federal office.
The transmission of electronic voting material across military networks.
Virtual private networks, cryptographic voting systems, centrally controlled voting stations, and other information security techniques.
The transmission of ballot representations and scanned pictures in a secure manner.
Capturing, retaining, and comparing electronic and physical ballot representations.
Utilization of voting stations at military bases.
Document delivery and upload systems.
The functional effectiveness of the application or adoption of the pilot program to operational environments, taking into account environmental and logistical obstacles and State procedures.
for the conduct of additional pilot programs under this section; and
for such legislation and administrative action as the Presidential designee determines appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Election Assistance Commission and the National Institute of Standards and Technology shall provide the Presidential designee with best practices or standards in accordance with electronic absentee voting guidelines established under the first sentence of section 1604(a)(2) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2002 (; ; note [now note]), as amended by section 567 of the Ronald W. Reagan National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2005 (; ) to support the pilot program or programs.Public Law 107–107115 Stat. 127742 U.S.C. 1973ff52 U.S.C. 20301Public Law 108–375118 Stat. 1919

children.label: A
children.text: The reasons such guidelines have not been established as of such date.

children.label: B
children.text: A detailed timeline for the establishment of such guidelines.

children.label: C
children.text: A detailed explanation of the Commission’s actions in establishing such guidelines since .October 28, 2004

children.label: A
children.text: the Committees on Appropriations, Armed Services, and Rules and Administration of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committees on Appropriations, Armed Services, and House Administration of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.
